articles.title: Freedom Comes at a Price: It Now Costs Nearly $8K a Year To Insure a New Teen Driver
articles.snippet: The Zebra's annual teen driving report finds insuring a new 16-year-old on a standalone policy now tops $7,900 a year — and explains why staying on a parent's policy saves thousands.

articles.title: Why Teen Driver Insurance Averages $5,700 a Year
articles.snippet: Adding a 16-year-old to a married couple's policy raises the premium by about $3,225 a year. Bankrate breaks down what's driving teen rates and how to blunt the hit.

articles.title: Car Insurance Is Up in 2026 — Here's What's Driving the Spike and What You Can Do To Save
articles.snippet: Repair costs, severe weather, and pricier vehicles keep pushing premiums up. Here's what's behind the 2026 increases — and how drivers are fighting back.

articles.title: The Average Cost of Car Insurance in 2026
articles.snippet: A state-by-state look at what drivers pay in 2026, why New York tops the list near $4,000 a year, and where coverage stays cheapest.

articles.title: Car Insurance Premium Inequities Are Driven by Age, Credit and ZIP Code, Study Finds
articles.snippet: A new study finds identical drivers can pay vastly different rates based on age, credit-based insurance scores, and ZIP code — reigniting the fairness debate.

articles.title: Top Ways To Save on Car Insurance in 2026
articles.snippet: From raising your deductible to stacking discounts and going usage-based, here are the highest-impact moves to cut your premium this year.

articles.title: What Is Uninsured Motorist Coverage? Guide for 2026
articles.snippet: With roughly 1 in 7 drivers uninsured, this guide explains how UM/UIM works, what it costs, and when it's worth adding.

articles.title: After Falling 6% in 2025, Average Auto Insurance Cost Will Stabilize in 2026, Says Insurify
articles.snippet: After a rare 6% drop in 2025, Insurify projects the average full-coverage premium will edge up just 1% to about $2,158 in 2026 — with a widening gap between states.

articles.title: Cheapest Car Insurance Companies of 2026
articles.snippet: U.S. News ranks the most affordable national insurers for 2026 and shows how much you can save by comparing at least three quotes.

articles.title: Cheap Car Insurance Companies — From $41/mo
articles.snippet: NerdWallet's latest analysis of the cheapest widely available insurers, with minimum-coverage rates starting around $41 a month.

articles.title: States With the Biggest Auto Insurance Price Changes in 2026
articles.snippet: Some states saw rates jump over 100% while others fell. See where 2026 premiums rose and dropped the most.

articles.title: Average Car Insurance Rates by Age and Gender (2026)
articles.snippet: How premiums fall as drivers age, why 16-year-olds pay the most, and the seven states that no longer let insurers use gender in pricing.

articles.title: Cheapest Liability-Only Car Insurance (2026)
articles.snippet: For older, paid-off cars, liability-only can start around $36 a month. See which insurers are cheapest and when dropping full coverage makes sense.

articles.title: How Does Your Credit Score Affect Auto Insurance Rates in 2026?
articles.snippet: Drivers with poor credit can pay nearly double for full coverage — except in the four states that ban the practice. Here's how to improve your insurance score.

articles.title: What Affects Car Insurance Rates? Factors, Data and How To Save
articles.snippet: A data-driven rundown of the dozen-plus factors insurers weigh — and which ones you can actually control.

articles.title: Car Insurance Prices Tumbled 6% in 2025 — Insurify Projects an Affordability Gap Between States in 2026
articles.snippet: Insurify's 2026 forecast: prices stabilize nationally but diverge sharply, with some states posting triple-digit jumps and others falling.
